utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: 1) health_coverage requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan includes physical and mental health descriptions and both monthly and annual costs, so fully specified. 2) property_coverage requires home/contents insurance with costs. The plan includes home_contents, coverage_details, and references monthly/annual costs (included in total), so specified with costs. 3) travel_coverage requires travel insurance with costs. The plan includes coverage_type, details, and costs (included in total), so specified. 4) additional_services requires any enhancement services with costs. The plan lists services and indicates monthly/annual costs (included in total), so specified. 5) total_budget requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. The plan provides current and optimized monthly and annual costs and savings, so fully specified. Therefore, all 5/5 items are included and fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists only generic, bundled descriptions like 'Enhanced executive health coverage,' 'Comprehensive property protection,' and 'Premium travel insurance' as part of a company benefits package, without naming any specific plans from the ground-truth list (e.g., EliteCare Premium Plan, PrimeHome Premium Coverage, EliteTravel Premium Plan). Because no explicit product names from the ground-truth ratings are included, there is no direct way to map any item in the final plan to a specific rated option.
